exactMethodClaim: A receipt-first benchmark triage score using baseline margin, holdout delta, split-key adequacy, recurrence potential, negative-control sanity, and source-family penalties predicts weak benchmark/data claims before deep validation better than random, source-family-only, task-size, and simple-baseline-only heuristics on bounded OpenML holdout tasks.
inputRequirements: concrete OpenML task ID and dataset ID
group/time/entity keys when the claim depends on them

components.name: baseline_margin
components.rationale: Claims with model-vs-baseline delta <= 0.04 repeatedly died as baseline_dominated.

components.name: holdout_delta
components.rationale: A fragility claim needs a nontrivial random-vs-group/time/entity holdout gap.

components.name: recurrence_potential
components.rationale: Single-task effects are demoted unless the same mechanism appears across independent tasks.

components.name: split_adequacy
components.rationale: Group/time/entity claims require documented keys and deterministic split manifests.

components.name: negative_control_sanity
components.rationale: Shuffled-target or negative controls must behave before deep validation.

components.name: source_family_penalty
components.rationale: Repeated same-family patterns are penalized unless supported by a concrete receipt and independent recurrence path.

rivalExplanations: The method only learned to reject all claims.
Task size explains all prediction quality.
Simple baseline margin alone explains prediction quality.
failureCases: Method ties or loses to simple-baseline-only heuristic.
Method advances a source-family-only or receipt-incomplete claim.
limitations: This is a bounded OpenML benchmark-methodology candidate, not a scientific discovery Fund.
The method is trained from a small negative HardSeed set and needs more receipt-complete holdouts before DiscoveryCandidate promotion.
